page: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1280s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 8 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, this configuration allocates shared CPU resources across 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 1280 GB NVMe SSD, while no GPU hardware is present.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory performance is a notable highlight, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the top 25%, supported by average read-only large block memory bandwidth of 127.29 GB/sec and write bandwidth of 75.3 GB/sec. Database and web serving operations, such as Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput, consistently register average tier results. However, LLM inference tasks show a performance tradeoff, with prompt processing on medium 7B models falling into the weak category at 23.58 tokens per second.

From a resource density perspective, this instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local NVMe storage, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The shared CPU allocation represents a tradeoff for sustained, high-intensity compute tasks, but the generous 64.0 GB RAM capacity and fast NVMe storage make it highly suitable for database hosting, caching layers, memory-intensive web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1280s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 1280 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server delivers a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU and memory performance, placing both in the top 25% tier, while multi-core compute, database operations, and static web serving throughput perform in the average range. The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained compute-heavy tasks, but the generous memory and fast local storage make this instance highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-rich virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and high-capacity local NVMe storage for database and caching workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
